board.verdict: The through-line advances on two fronts simultaneously. While the United States conducts direct military operations against Iran and a proxy war in Ukraine, China's May exports hit a record $376.78 billion with semiconductors up 66.5% — the sector decoupling was designed to suppress. Institutional defection from the Atlantic order is now running on the production ledger as much as the diplomatic one.
board.watching: BRICS Bridge from agenda item to implementation; Saudi non-dollar settlement; Qatari mediation in the Iran war.

board.verdict: Hegemonies do not fall, they leak. Iran tolls Hormuz in yuan at $2 million a vessel; Indian refiners settle Russian crude in yuan and dirhams; mBridge has moved $55 billion, 95% in digital yuan; reserves at 57.8% from 72% in 2001. The snapshot still favors the dollar. The direction does not.
board.watching: Reserve composition data; the next oil contract that prices in yuan.

board.verdict: Xi crossed the Yalu and 'denuclearization' did not survive the trip — absent from official Chinese summaries, which is policy, not oversight. Pyongyang now holds a position two patrons deep and sells its leverage at the top of the market. The first road bridge across the Tumen River opens June 19 — Russia operationalizes the second patron relationship logistically. Beijing no longer holds a monopoly on land access to the North.
board.watching: Traffic volumes on the Tumen bridge after June 19; Chinese customs response on the 1959 rail bridge; nuclear-state language at multilateral forums.

board.verdict: The architecture advances — unified force at 15,000, gold custody, franc-zone exit on schedule — but April's coordinated assault on Gao, Menaka, and Kidal showed the insurgency's reach, and the Russian guarantee failed in public view. Sovereignty first, then capacity. The second part is now urgent.
board.watching: Gold revenue allocated to the unified force post-Kidal; renegotiated security arrangements.

board.verdict: Fixed-asset investment negative for the first time in thirty years; property down 13.7%. But May 2026 exports hit $376.78 billion (+19.4% YoY), a record, and semiconductor exports surged 66.5%. The export machine is outrunning the property drag; the decoupling story is running against the grain of the numbers. The 15th Plan loads the productive-capacity ledger. The desk waits for the June FAI data.
board.watching: June manufacturing-investment data; whether semiconductor export growth compresses; any credit instrument aimed at the household side.

board.verdict: Trump named Kharg Island as a seizure target on June 11 — the threat that was supposed to remain implicit in the Qatar mediation track is now public doctrine. Iran's FM declared the ceasefire 'effectively meaningless.' The US naval blockade fired on a tanker, killing three Indian sailors. Trump then cancelled Thursday strikes, citing negotiations elevated to the highest levels of Iranian leadership. The United States is conducting a direct war against Iran, a proxy war in Ukraine, and managing a frozen peace track — the industrial and political base for all three simultaneously has not been demonstrated.
board.watching: Iran ceasefire extension or collapse; further naval blockade incidents; pledge-to-delivery ratio on military aid to Ukraine.

wars.basis: The desk states it plainly: Russia has been winning, is winning, and will win. Moscow made the tempo itself a weapon — it fights at the pace its industry sustains and its adversary's politics cannot. Russia's Zubr anti-drone system is now in serial production for mobile fire groups, filling a documented counter-UAV capability gap. Nine missiles struck Kyiv unintercepted — that fact is now being acknowledged by Western officers, not just analysts. Ukrainian deep-strike operations have reached 1,000 kilometres into Russian territory; these are real and should be recorded. They do not change the arithmetic. The Geneva trilateral peace format has been frozen since March, consumed by Washington's Iran bandwidth.

wars.advantage: Contested — US air superiority, Iran asymmetric
wars.basis: US and Israel initiated strikes on Iran on February 28, 2026. A ceasefire was declared April 8 and has been violated repeatedly by both sides. On June 11, Trump published on Truth Social that the US would 'seize...Kharg Island, and other oil infrastructure points, and assume total control of their Oil and Gas Markets.' This is the first time US intent to seize foreign oil infrastructure has been stated as operational doctrine in a live conflict. The US naval blockade fired on a merchant tanker, killing three Indian sailors, pulling a third-party casualty into the ledger. Iran's Foreign Ministry declared the ceasefire 'effectively meaningless.' Trump then cancelled planned Thursday strikes, saying negotiations had been elevated to the highest levels of Iranian leadership. The oil infrastructure has not been struck or seized — that read is unchanged — but the envelope of threats has now been stated at its full extent publicly. Any strike now occurs within a pre-announced frame. China has been stockpiling (May imports +24.5%).

AES states hold the cities, the budgets, and the gold; JNIM demonstrated operational reach with April's simultaneous assault and the killing of Mali's defense minister. The Russian guarantee failed visibly at Kidal. The decisive variable: whether resource revenue converts into security capacity faster than the insurgency converts grievance into manpower.

predictions.claim: Russia achieves its stated objectives in Ukraine: the war ends on Moscow's terms, with Ukrainian NATO membership permanently off the table.
'Denuclearization' does not return to official PRC language on the DPRK. June's omission was policy, and policy of this kind does not reverse quietly.
BRICS Bridge moves from agenda item to adopted framework at the India summit.
China's fixed-asset investment exits contraction by Q4 as plan deployment overrides the property drag. If it does not, the desk will say so in this column.

Spain lifts the World Cup on July 19 in New Jersey. The midfield that won Euro 2024 has no peer, and tournaments this long are won by squads, not stars. The desk does not do sentimentality, even over tea.

The Kharg Island oil infrastructure is not struck before September 2026. Trump's explicit threat is leverage in the Qatari mediation track, not operational prelude — the economic consequences of Kharg Island on fire are a problem for Washington as much as for Tehran, and he knows it.

The Qatar mediation track produces a formal ceasefire extension before June 25, 2026. Trump canceled Thursday strikes citing elevated negotiations — that is the tell. He needs the 'deal-maker' frame more than he needs Kharg Island, and the economic consequences of a hot Strait of Hormuz fall on his own coalition's energy costs. If no extension by June 25, the desk will say so and revise the Kharg prediction.

items.text: Trump names the island: Kharg seizure threat, ceasefire 'meaningless,' negotiations elevated
The Tumen road: Russia-DPRK first road bridge opens June 19, second land corridor for Pyongyang
Ukraine — the pace that wins: Zubr serial production, NATO doctrine gap acknowledged, Geneva talks frozen

watching: Iran ceasefire extension or collapse (before June 25)
DPRK-Russia bridge traffic and Beijing customs response
AES gold-revenue allocation to unified force

items.text: Two blows to the logistics chain: Davydov car bomb, Kuibyshev refinery offline
Kharg Island, spared for now: US strikes military targets, threatens oil infrastructure
Beijing's May numbers: exports +19.4%, semiconductors +66.5%, narrative loses again
The standing reference reads the air defense ledger: Hodges confirms 9/9 Kyiv unintercepted

watching: Iran war ceasefire and Kharg Island oil infrastructure
China June trade and FAI data
DPRK nuclear-state language at multilateral forums
AES gold-revenue allocation to unified force

items.text: Xi crosses the Yalu, again — and declines to mention the bomb
Kidal falls, and the AES counts its dead (April events — filed stale; see Corrections)

watching: DPRK nuclear recognition at multilateral forums
BRICS Bridge at the India summit

corrections.text: The desk filed April's Kidal events as the morning's news — six weeks stale. The reader caught it; the desk did not. A freshness rule now stands in the standing orders: news means the last 48 hours, older events appear only as context.
